title: eupatorium maculatum subsp. bruneri
intro: Eupatorium maculatum subsp. bruneri, also known as Joe-Pye weed, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Eupatorium maculatum subsp. bruneri include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and soothe digestive issues, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Joe-Pye weed include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
